summary: The job failed because a shell conditional in the workflow evaluated to false and executed exit 1, causing the job to terminate with a non-zero exit code.
root_cause: A workflow shell conditional evaluated to false and ran 'exit 1', causing the job to fail.

evidence.quote:
if [[ "failure" == "success" || "failure" == "skipped" ]] && 
   [[ "skipped" == "success" || "skipped" == "skipped" ]]; then
  echo "OK"
else
  exit 1
fi
evidence.reason: Shows the exact shell conditional and that the else branch contains 'exit 1', which will fail the job when the condition is false.
Identifies the job that was running when the failing conditional was executed.
Demonstrates the job terminated with a non-zero exit code as a result of the script's 'exit 1'.

suggested_fix: Fix the workflow conditional so it evaluates to true when intended (use the proper status variables/values instead of the shown hardcoded strings), or remove/adjust the 'exit 1' fallback. For example, replace the hardcoded comparisons in the if statement with the correct GitHub Actions status context variables so the condition reflects the actual job statuses.
